image.alt: A map of Florida showing the streamflow drought status at streamgage locations. One gage is selected, and a window in the bottom left shows a timeseries of observed and forecast streamflow conditions at that site
An abstract compilation of snippets from charts that illustrate how streamflow drought is forecast

items.title: Water availability in the United States

image.alt: Map showing water limitation in the lower 48 United States by watershed. The background visualization shows monthly dynamics in water limitation from 2010 to 2020.
Six tiles arranged in a grid of 3 columns and 2 rows show thumbnails for visualizations in the Earth in Flux chart gallery

items.title: Managing redband trout for climate resilience

image.alt: Illustrated river with two cartoon redband trout swimming in it in front of a data visualization that shows how cold- and warm-adapted redband trout are affected by water temperature. The visualization demonstrates that cold-adapted redband trout suffer from thermal stress at lower overall temperatures than warm-adapted redband trout. Text on the visualization includes labels for each line: "cold-adapted redband trout" and "warm-adapted redband trout" and a label for "thermal stress."

Two contrasting images of water sources with the top image showing a close-up of a hand holding a clear glass being filled with water from a kitchen faucet. The bottom image shows a rustic landscape with a blue well tank labeled WELL-X-TROL with a wired fence and dry grass in the background.
A map of Texas showing the count of bottled water facilities in each county.
An image of dried, cracked mud overlain by a chart with long vertical bands representing drought events
An illustration of a partially dried-up streambed, wilted sunflower, and brown landscape depicting streamflow drought, which follows after meteorological and agricultural drought.
